segments.text: Then Job replied:
How long will you torment me and crush me with words?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Ten times now you have reproached me; shamelessly you attack me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If it is true that I have gone astray, my error remains my concern alone.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If indeed you would exalt yourselves above me and use my humiliation against me,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: then know that God has wronged me and drawn his net around me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Though I cry, 'Violence!' I get no response; though I call for help, there is no justice.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He has blocked my way so I cannot pass; he has shrouded my paths in darkness.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He has stripped me of my honor and removed the crown from my head.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He tears me down on every side till I am gone; he uproots my hope like a tree.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: His anger burns against me; he counts me among his enemies.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: His troops advance in force; they build a siege ramp against me and encamp around my tent.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He has alienated my family from me; my acquaintances are completely estranged from me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My relatives have gone away; my closest friends have forgotten me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My guests and my female servants count me a foreigner; they look on me as on a stranger.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I summon my servant, but he does not answer, though I beg him with my own mouth.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My breath is offensive to my wife; I am loathsome to my own family.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Even the little boys scorn me; when I appear, they ridicule me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: All my intimate friends detest me; those I love have turned against me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I am nothing but skin and bones; I have escaped only by the skin of my teeth.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Have pity on me, my friends, have pity, for the hand of God has struck me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Why do you pursue me as God does? Will you never get enough of my flesh?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Oh, that my words were recorded, that they were written on a scroll,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: that they were inscribed with an iron tool on lead, or engraved in rock forever!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I know that my redeemer lives, and that in the end he will stand on the earth.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: And after my skin has been destroyed, yet in my flesh I will see God;
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I myself will see him with my own eyes—I, and not another. How my heart yearns within me!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If you say, 'How we will hound him, since the root of the trouble lies in him,'
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: you should fear the sword yourselves; for wrath will bring punishment by the sword, and then you will know that there is judgment.
segments.speaker: Job
